title: Quality Gate Orchestration, Failure Semantics, and Required Checks · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Skipped Cancelled Neutral Failure
summary: A pipeline runs formatter, lint, type, tests, and build under one continue-on-error step, reports green after skipped work, and protects the branch with a mutable job name. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci skipped cancelled neutral failure increment in quality gate dependency graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish success, failure, cancelled, skipped, neutral, timed out, and continued failures in job and required-check policy.
Order cheap deterministic gates before costly work while parallelizing independent evidence and preserving causal diagnosis.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example pipeline hides formatter, lint, type, test, and build failures behind continue-on-error and skipped jobs while branch rules require a renamed check. Rebuild the dependency graph and evidence ownership. Competency probe: Distinguish success, failure, cancelled, skipped, neutral, timed out, and continued failures in job and required-check policy. A defensible solution applies Ci Skipped Cancelled Neutral Failure, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to quality gate dependency graph.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-ci-quality-gate-orchestration-3
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-quality-gate-orchestration-3
    artifact: quality-pipeline.yaml
    decision: worked ci quality gate orchestration 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      gates: [format-check, lint, type-check, unit, integration, build, policy]
      order: cheap-before-costly
      parallel: independent-only
      continue-on-error: false
      skipped-policy: explicit-failure
      required-check: stable-quality-name
      summary: command-revision-owner
      rerun: classified-only
    verify: inspect job results required checks skipped paths exact commands and retained failure summaries
    failure: Reject green continued failures, silent skips, mutable required names, log dumps, or unconditional reruns.
    repair: Make exit semantics explicit, preserve dependency order, fail unintended skips, stabilize checks, and assign diagnostic ownership.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
